title: Censored Time-to-Next-Stop Hazard Stack
summary: Official XGBoost AFT guidance matches this setup: time-to-event with censoring.
rationale: Official XGBoost AFT guidance matches this setup: time-to-event with censoring. Many rows are not immediate positives, but they still contain signal about how close the next stop is; modeling only next-lap binary labels discards that ordering information.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race sequence, derive time_until_next_pit; censor rows with no later pit at race end. Train an AFT survival model on leakage-safe online features, then convert predicted survival to a discrete next-lap hazard or a short-horizon hazard vector. Stack that OOF hazard with the main classifier rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Should add smoother stint-end pressure estimates and reduce brittle threshold behavior around the current heuristic finish-window features.
risk: Label construction is easy to leak if future pits are used inside feature engineering rather than only for the auxiliary target; conversion from survival outputs to next-lap probability must be done consistently.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
